question: Illustrate the importance of virtual destructors. Create a base class `Base` and a derived class `Derived`, both with print statements in their constructors and destructors. Demonstrate memory leakage when deleting a `Derived` object through a `Base` class pointer without a virtual destructor, and then fix it.

test_cases.expected:
--- Demonstrating Correct Polymorphic Destruction (Raw Pointer) ---
Creating a Derived object via a Base pointer:
Base Constructor
Derived Constructor (Raw Pointer Managed Derived)

Deleting the object via the Base pointer:
Derived Destructor (Raw Pointer Managed Derived)
Base Destructor
--- End Raw Pointer Demonstration ---

--- Demonstrating Correct Polymorphic Destruction (Smart Pointer) ---
Creating a Derived object using std::unique_ptr<Base>:
Base Constructor
Derived Constructor (Smart Pointer Managed Derived)
Smart pointer object created. It will be automatically deleted on scope exit.
--- End Smart Pointer Demonstration (Scope Exit) ---
Derived Destructor (Smart Pointer Managed Derived)
Base Destructor
